solution.p: We will factor 100r^{2}-60ry+9y^{2} where a=100, b=-60, and c=9.

Perfect Square Formula: The formula for perfect square trinomials is (a \pm b)^2=a^2 \pm 2ab + b^2. Note that the polynomial 100r^{2}-60ry+9y^{2} matches the formula's right side, and we have: \begin{aligned}[t]100r^{2}-60ry+9y^{2}\amp =\left(10r\right)^2-2\left(10r\right)\left(3y\right)+\left(3y\right)^2\newline \amp =\left(10r-3y\right)\mathopen{}\left(10r-3y\right)\newline \amp =\left(10r-3y\right)^{2}\end{aligned}
AC Method: In this method, we first calculate the product of a and c in ax^2+bx+c. For this problem, we have (100) \cdot (9) = 900.

The number 900 can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways:  egin{array}{lll}1(900)\amp -1(-900)\amp 2(450)\amp -2(-450)
ewline3(300)\amp -3(-300)\amp 4(225)\amp -4(-225)
ewline5(180)\amp -5(-180)\amp 6(150)\amp -6(-150)
ewline9(100)\amp -9(-100)\amp 10(90)\amp -10(-90)
ewline12(75)\amp -12(-75)\amp 15(60)\amp -15(-60)
ewline18(50)\amp -18(-50)\amp 20(45)\amp -20(-45)
ewline25(36)\amp -25(-36)\amp 30(30)\amp -30(-30)
ewline\end{array} Looking at the corresponding sums, we want a sum of -60, and this sum happens with (-30)+(-30)=-60 So we write  egin{aligned}[t]100r^{2}-60ry+9y^{2}\amp =100r^{2}-30ry-30ry+9y^{2}
ewline \amp =10r\mathopen{}\left(10r-3yight)-3y\mathopen{}\left(10r-3yight)
ewline \amp =\left(10r-3yight)\mathopen{}\left(10r-3yight)
ewline \amp =\left(10r-3yight)^{2}\end{aligned}Generic Rectangles Method: First, we set up generic rectangles by putting the first term and third term in 100r^{2}-60ry+9y^{2} into the top left and bottom right rectangles:

The first term's coefficient, 100, can be factored into the product of two numbers (where the first factor is positive) in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(100)\amp 2(50)\amp 4(25)\amp 5(20)\newline10(10)\amp \end{array}
The third term's coefficient, 9, can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(9)\amp -1(-9)\amp 3(3)\amp -3(-3)\newline\end{array}

We put each pair into the corresponding places next to those generic rectangles, and try to match the area of those rectangles with 100r^{2}-60ry+9y^{2} by guess-and-check. The following generic rectangles show the solution:

Dimensions of those generic rectangles in the diagram give us the solution: \begin{aligned}[t]100r^{2}-60ry+9y^{2}\amp =\left(10r-3y\right)\mathopen{}\left(10r-3y\right)\newline \amp =\left(10r-3y\right)^{2}\end{aligned}
